commit.message:
Update components/ui/common/CheckBoxWithLabel.tsx

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Build lawful-git: data-driven git guardrail engine (#1)

* Initial plan

* Implement lawful-git: engine, config, tests, installers, README

Co-authored-by: asklar <22989529+asklar@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add .gitignore, fix batched dirty check, clarify loadConfig passthrough comment

Co-authored-by: asklar <22989529+asklar@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add CONTRIBUTING.md and .github/copilot-instructions.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix edge cases, add CI/release workflows, /release skill, and test coverage

Bug fixes in main.go:
- Strip + prefix from refspecs in parsePushTarget (bypassed protected_branches)
- Match subcommands by first positional arg, not rest[0] (flag bypass)
- Respect -- separator in positionalArgs (dash-prefixed file bypass)
- Propagate --git-dir and --work-tree to subprocess context
- Fail closed on malformed .git-safety.json
- Add --lawful-version flag with build-time version injection

Test improvements:
- Replace cd $REPO with cd $TMPDIR_ROOT for isolation
- Add before/after source repo integrity check
- Add tests: require_upstream_before_bare_push, push --delete,
  +refspec, subcommand bypass, malformed config, --no-verify on
  cherry-pick/merge/am, --lawful-version (48 tests total)

CI/CD:
- Add ci.yml (build + test on Linux/macOS/Windows)
- Add release.yml (cross-compile + GitHub Release on tag push)
- Add /release skill for semver tag creation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix checkout dirty check for dash-prefixed files, add 8 edge case tests

Bug fix: check_dirty_on_checkout now includes dash-prefixed filenames
after the -- separator (same pattern fixed earlier in positionalArgs).

New test cases (56 total):
- git tag --delete (long form)
- git checkout -b blocked by worktree_only
- git add --all (long form)
- git restore -S (short form)
- git --no-pager push --force (global flag + blocked command)
- git push origin main:main (refspec syntax + protected_branches)
- empty config {} passthrough
- git add -- . (blocked path after separator)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rename example folder from vhagar to my-project

The folder name is just an example of a scoped path prefix —
my-project is self-explanatory for anyone reading the config or tests.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Validate config: reject absolute path prefixes in scoped_paths and protected_branches

Paths in git are relative to the repo root, so prefixes like '/src/'
can never match and would silently block everything. loadConfig() now
rejects these with a clear error message.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Comprehensive config validation: reject typos, bad types, and nonsensical rules

- Reject unknown/misspelled JSON keys via DisallowUnknownFields
  (e.g. 'worktree_only_branch' typo silently disabled the feature)
- Require non-empty command and message on all rule types
- Require non-empty one_of_flags in require rules (empty = unsatisfiable)
- Reject flag_in_bundle values that aren't exactly 1 character
- Reject flag values that don't start with '-'
- Reject absolute path prefixes (already existed, now part of unified validation)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add cross-rule and structural config validation

New validations:
- Subcommand starting with '-' (never matches positional args)
- Path traversal '..' in allowed_prefixes/blocked_paths
- Require rule on a bare-blocked command (dead code)
- Unsatisfiable require: all one_of_flags also blocked for same command

Refactored path validation into shared validatePathPrefix().
66 tests passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: consent mechanism with repo/branch context in dialogs

Add action: consent for soft-blocking rules that allow agent override
with justification. Two-step flow: first attempt prints instructions to
write justification to temp file, retry triggers native dialog or
external consent_command.

Dialog shows repo path, current branch, exact command, rule message,
and agent justification. Supports Windows (MessageBox), macOS
(osascript), Linux (zenity), and terminal fallback.

Consent file uses deterministic hash of repo+args for uniqueness.
File is deleted after single use.

Includes config validation for action field and 5 new tests (71 total).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: add consent denial, empty justification, and JSON payload validation tests

- consent_command that exits non-zero blocks the operation
- empty justification file is rejected with clear error
- consent_command receives correct JSON with message, justification,
  args, repo, and branch fields
- 74 tests total (3 new consent tests)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: show Windows MessageBox from WSL via powershell.exe

Detect WSL by checking /proc/version for 'microsoft'/'wsl'. When on
WSL with powershell.exe available, show a native Windows MessageBox
instead of falling back to terminal. Falls through to zenity then
terminal if unavailable.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: PowerShell dialog newlines rendered literally

backtick-n is only interpreted in double-quoted PowerShell strings.
Switch MessageBox.Show() arguments from single to double quotes in
both Windows and WSL dialog functions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: delete consent file immediately, not via defer

os.Exit() in block() skips deferred functions, so the consent file
survived when the user denied consent or justification was empty.
Move os.Remove() before the approval check so it always runs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: verify consent file cleanup after denial and empty justification

Adds assertions that the consent file is deleted in all exit paths:
- After consent_command denies (exit non-zero)
- After empty justification file is rejected
76 tests total.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: document consent mechanism and custom consent_command in README

Add consent section covering the two-step flow, platform-specific
dialogs (Windows, WSL, macOS, Linux, terminal fallback), custom
consent_command with JSON payload format, and the action field on
blocked rules.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: replace flag/flag_in_bundle with flags array

Merge the separate 'flag' and 'flag_in_bundle' fields into a single
'flags' array on blocked rules. Each entry is matched as an exact arg;
short flags like '-f' also match inside bundled args like '-xvf'.

This eliminates the need for two rules to block --force and -f:
  Before: two rules with flag and flag_in_bundle
  After:  one rule with flags: ["--force", "-f"]

Breaking change (pre-release, no users yet).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: normalize repo path to OS-native separators in consent dialog

Apply filepath.FromSlash to repoRoot so Windows displays paths with
backslashes instead of forward slashes in consent dialogs and JSON
payloads. Consent payload test now validates the full repo path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: point to config docs on validation errors

When .git-safety.json has errors, print a link to the configuration
reference in the README so users can fix their config. All config
validation errors (unknown fields, missing fields, invalid values)
flow through a single error path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: validate consent_command path exists at config load

Use exec.LookPath to verify consent_command resolves to an executable.
Invalid paths fail at config load rather than silently at consent time.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: verify config errors include docs link and consent_command details

- Config validation errors show the configuration-reference URL
- Invalid consent_command error names the bad path
- Invalid consent_command error includes docs link
80 tests total.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: support JSONC comments in .git-safety.json

Strip // line comments and /* */ block comments before JSON decoding.
Comments inside quoted strings are preserved. Zero dependencies —
uses a simple state-machine parser.

4 new tests: line comments, block comments, inline block comments,
and comments-inside-strings preservation. 84 tests total.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: evaluate all hard rules before prompting for consent

Defer consent rules until after require, scoped_paths, worktree,
upstream, and protected_branches checks. This prevents wasting the
user's time on a consent dialog when a different rule would block
the command anyway.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: embed Windows manifest for comctl32 v6 visual styles

Add lawful-git.manifest requesting Microsoft.Windows.Common-Controls
v6.0.0.0 and pre-built .syso resource files for amd64 and arm64.
Go's linker embeds these automatically on Windows builds, giving
the consent MessageBox modern visual styles.

Also add windows-arm64 to the release matrix.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: direct Win32 MessageBoxW syscall, env var for console consent

Replace PowerShell-based MessageBox on Windows with direct
MessageBoxW syscall via user32.dll. Runs in-process so the
embedded manifest applies comctl32 v6 visual styles.

Platform dialog logic split into build-tagged files:
- dialog_windows.go: direct Win32 MessageBoxW
- dialog_other.go: WSL (powershell.exe), macOS (osascript),
  Linux (zenity), terminal fallback

Set LAWFUL_GIT_CONSOLE_CONSENT=1 to force terminal y/N prompt
on any platform.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: global per-user config with merge semantics

Add support for ~/.lawful-git.json (or LAWFUL_GIT_GLOBAL_CONFIG env var)
as a global config that applies to all repos. When both global and repo
configs exist, they're merged:

- Arrays (blocked, require, scoped_paths): union
- Maps (protected_branches): union, repo wins on key conflict
- Booleans: OR logic (either enabling turns it on)
- consent_command: repo wins if both define it

Validation runs on the merged result. Global config applies even
outside repos for rules that don't need repo context.

7 new tests (92 total): global-only, merge union, boolean OR,
consent_command override, protected_branches merge.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: harden edge cases, update docs, add tests

Bug fixes:
- Config permission denied now fails closed (was silent passthrough)
- Scoped paths normalize ./prefix to prevent bypass
- Terminal consent buffer increased from 8 to 64 bytes
- Consent file capped at 1MB to prevent OOM

Documentation:
- README: updated intro for global config, added --lawful-version,

- Scoped path ./prefix bypass prevented
- Scoped path ./allowed prefix works

Bug fix: blocked_paths check now normalizes with filepath.Clean,

New tests (98 total):
- blocked_paths ./. normalization
- LAWFUL_GIT_CONSOLE_CONSENT doesn't break consent flow
- --lawful-version was already tested (removed duplicate)

commit.message:
Fix `bun run dev:desktop` from not terminating properly on SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP (#305)

* fix: handle turbo shutdown in dev runner

* refactor: rely on effect child process shutdown

* refactor: inline turbo shutdown timeout

* add back sigint sigterm sighup handling

* refactor: tighten turbo signal forwarding

* refactor: inline dev runner errors

* fix: use detached false instead of manual signal forwarding

Effect defaults to detached: true on non-Windows, putting turbo in a
new process group where terminal signals (Ctrl+C) never reach it.
Setting detached: false keeps turbo in the same group, eliminating
the need for ~130 lines of manual signal forwarding.

* Update scripts/dev-runner.ts

perf/a11y: memoize sections, fix list keys, add slider focus style

- Wrap all 16 section components with React.memo() to prevent
  unnecessary re-renders caused by App.jsx state changes
  (scroll position, activeSection, dismissedCTA, etc.)
- Replace key={index} with stable content-derived keys in 13+
  map() calls across HeroSection, AuditSection, CompetitorsSection,
  DocuSignSection, PlanSection, TourRecoverySnapshotSection, and
  WireframesSection
- Add useMemo() to IssuesSection (counts + filtered) and
  CompetitorsSection (filtered) to avoid re-computing on every render
- Hoist progressSections.findIndex() out of render map loops in
  App.jsx (was called 32+ times per render; now computed once)
- Add :focus-visible outline to .roi-slider in index.css so
  keyboard users see a visible focus indicator on the ROI range input

> Requests with Manael transform parameters are currently forwarded to
upstream as-is.
> 
> `w`, `h`, `fit` (and similarly transform-only params such as `q`) are
parsed by Manael, but remain on the outbound upstream URL.
> 
> ## Current behavior
> - `ServeHTTP` reads query params for transform options.
> - Request is then proxied without removing transform-only params.
> - Upstream receives those params.
> 
> ## Why this is a problem
> - Leaks internal transform controls to origin.
> - Can create unnecessary cache key fragmentation at origin/CDN.
> - May trigger unintended behavior if origin uses same param names.
>

> Manael-specific transform parameters should be consumed by Manael and
**not** forwarded upstream.
> Non-Manael query params should continue to be forwarded unchanged.
> 
> ## Reproduction
> 1. Run Manael with any upstream that can echo request URI/query.
> 2. Request an image via Manael, e.g.:
>    - `/photo.jpeg?w=200&h=200&fit=cover`
> 3. Observe upstream receives `w/h/fit` in query string.
> 
> ## Proposed fix direction
> - Before proxying, clone request URL query and remove transform
params:
>   - resize: `w`, `h`, `fit`
> - quality: `q` (and any format-specific quality keys supported by
Manael)
> - Keep all unrelated query params intact.
> - Add tests that verify upstream does not receive Manael transform
params while preserving unrelated params.
> 
> ## Acceptance criteria
> - Upstream no longer receives Manael transform-only query params.
> - Existing transform behavior remains unchanged.
> - Non-transform query params still pass through.
> - Unit/integration tests cover this behavior.

commit.message:
Fix debug APK package conflict: unconditional signing + applicationIdSuffix (#55)

* Initial plan

* Fix package conflict: remove if-guard from signingConfig, add applicationIdSuffix for debug builds

- Remove the conditional `if (debugKeystorePath.exists())` guard from
  signingConfigs.debug. The guard silently fell back to Android's SDK-generated
  debug keystore (different on each runner VM) when the file wasn't found,
  causing INSTALL_FAILED_UPDATE_INCOMPATIBLE. The committed keystore is now
  always used unconditionally; if it's missing the build fails loudly.

- Add applicationIdSuffix ".debug" to buildTypes.debug, giving debug APKs
  the package ID com.hyliankid14.bbcradioplayer.debug. This prevents conflicts
  when a release build (F-Droid / Play Store) is already installed on the
  same device — they now have different package identifiers so they can
  coexist and be updated independently.
